hero.headline: London Student Rentals Near Western University
hero.subhead: Cash-flowing investment properties minutes from Western University, targeting a student tenant pool of 42,000+ with limited on-campus housing.

page_plan.story_md:
London, Ontario is one of Canada's largest university towns, and Western University sits at the centre of its rental economy. With 42,000+ students competing for a limited pool of off-campus beds, purpose-built student rentals near campus offer investors an unusually consistent tenant pipeline year over year.

These properties are positioned for investors who want cash flow on day one rather than long-hold appreciation alone. The opportunity is built around the gap between Western's enrolment and its on-campus supply — Western's Off-Campus Housing team posts over 6,000 off-campus rental listings annually and is the #1 source for Western students to find off-campus housing, signalling just how much of the student body the private market absorbs every September.

London is also a structurally tight rental market. London is home to both Western University and Fanshawe College, creating one of the largest student populations in Ontario, and with limited on-campus housing, demand for rentals is consistently strong. That dynamic supports both occupancy and rent growth for well-located, properly licensed student homes.

Purchasers tour available properties directly with the builder on June 13, 2026, and can opt into in-house property management — a turnkey path for out-of-town investors who want exposure to the London student market without managing tenants, leases, or licensing themselves.

selling_points.title: Western University tenant base
selling_points.body: Properties target the 42,000+ student population at Western, a globally ranked research university whose off-campus demand consistently outstrips supply.

selling_points.title: Structural housing shortage
selling_points.body: On-campus beds cover only a fraction of enrolment, pushing the majority of students into the private rental market each academic year.

selling_points.title: Designed for immediate cash flow
selling_points.body: Layouts and bedroom counts are configured for student-by-the-room leasing, which typically produces higher gross rents than single-family tenancy.

selling_points.title: In-house property management
selling_points.body: Optional management handles tenant placement, rent collection, and licensing compliance, giving out-of-town investors a hands-off ownership experience.

selling_points.title: Buy direct from the builder
selling_points.body: The June 13, 2026 tour gives purchasers a chance to walk the properties and negotiate directly with the developer rather than through resale channels.

selling_points.title: Bonus VIP incentives
selling_points.body: Additional purchaser incentives are released at the tour for buyers who transact during the VIP launch window.

incentives.title: Bonus VIP purchaser incentives (released at tour)

location.blurb: Properties are positioned within the Western University catchment in London, Ontario — a market where student demand consistently exceeds on-campus supply and the private rental market absorbs thousands of leases each year.

faq.q: When can I tour the properties?
faq.a: The builder is hosting an on-site tour on June 13, 2026, where purchasers can walk available properties and meet the developer directly.
Undergraduate and graduate students from Western University, which enrolls 42,000+ students competing for a limited supply of off-campus housing each year.

faq.q: Do I have to manage the property myself?
faq.a: No. In-house property management is available, covering tenant placement and day-to-day operations so the investment can be run hands-off.

faq.q: Are these properties designed for cash flow or appreciation?
faq.a: Both. Layouts are configured to generate immediate rental cash flow, while the London/Western market also offers long-term appreciation potential in a structurally undersupplied rental market.

faq.q: What incentives are available at the VIP launch?
faq.a: Bonus purchaser incentives are released to buyers who attend the tour. Specific details are disclosed by the builder on June 13, 2026.

meta.title: London Student Rentals — Western University Investment | VIP Launch
meta.description: VIP launch of cash-flowing student rental properties near Western University in London, ON. Builder tour June 13, 2026 — in-house management available.

hero.headline: Sol Stacked Towns — Waterdown From $399,990
hero.subhead: Boutique 21-unit stacked townhome community by New Horizon, blending Waterdown's small-town charm with quick QEW, Hwy 403 and Aldershot GO access.

page_plan.story_md:
Sol Stacked Towns is a new pre-construction stacked townhome community by New Horizon Development Group, coming soon to 90 Skinner Road, Hamilton, Ontario. The boutique site sits in Waterdown East, one of Hamilton's desirable residential areas — a short drive from Aldershot GO, the QEW, and Highway 403, which puts Burlington, Hamilton and downtown Toronto squarely within commuter reach.

The collection is intentionally small: 21 Townhouses over 3 Storeys in two product types. The 1-storey **Rhythm** is a 672 sq ft, 1-bed / 1-bath layout priced from $399,990, and the 2-storey **Radiant** is a 1,362 sq ft, 3-bed / 2.5-bath floor plan from $649,990. SOL offers three-bedroom floor plans with 2.5 bathrooms, contemporary finishes, gourmet kitchens, private garages, and large outdoor terraces.

For investors, the math is unusually friendly for a 2026 GTHA launch. No HST for all buyers — the builder's current project page highlights a limited-time "NO HST FOR ALL BUYERS" offer, with pricing adjusted for the enhanced HST rebate. Development charges are capped at $0, assignments are free, and the deposit is spread across four installments through 180 days — a total of $45,000 (1-storey) or $70,000 (2-storey). Monthly condo fees are $246, covering exterior maintenance, landscaping, snow removal and insurance on common elements.

The builder is a known quantity in Southern Ontario. The project is developed by New Horizon Development Group, a respected Southern Ontario builder active since 1993. With only 21 doors, Sol is positioned as a low-density, end-user-friendly alternative to the higher-priced Burlington and Oakville stacked-town market.

selling_points.body: 1-storey Rhythm suites start at $399,990 and 2-storey Radiant townhomes from $649,990 — meaningfully below comparable Burlington and Oakville stacked towns.

Yes — the developer is offering a free assignment clause (refer to APS for details), which is notable for investors planning to flip before final closing.
